Language: en

## Story

Team News: Sweden vs. Tunisia injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026 - Sports Mole

Menu

World Cup Gameweek 1

Monterrey Stadium

# Team News: Sweden vs. Tunisia injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Ben Knapton| 14 Jun 2026 02:00, Last updated: 14 Jun 2026 02:00

© Imago / TT

Sweden and Tunisia's paths to the 2026 World Cup could hardly have been more contrasting, but their methods of qualification will matter little when their Group F clash gets underway in Guadalupe in the early hours of Monday morning.

Blagult earned their ticket to North America via the playoffs after failing to win a single group game in Qualifying, whereas their African foes earned nine preliminary victories from a possible nine.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both nations.

---

## SWEDEN vs. TUNISIA

## SWEDEN

Out: None

Doubtful: Gabriel Gudmundsson(illness)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Nordfeldt; Hien, Lindelof, Lagerbielke; Bernhardsson, Kar

news_items.title: Sweden vs Tunisia team news and predicted lineups
news_items.text:
# Sweden vs Tunisia team news and predicted lineups

Published: 2026-06-12T11:41:31+00:00
Source: squawka.com (squawka.com)
Language: en

## Story

Sweden get their 2026 World Cup campaign underway when they face Tunisia on Sunday at 3am (UK time).

Sweden are at the World Cup for the 13th time and are back after being absent in 2022. They reached the quarter-finals on their last appearance, and haven’t failed to make it out of the group stage since 1990. But that does also include more failures to qualify in 1998, 2010 and 2014.

Tunisia, meanwhile, have matched their best run of World Cup appearances with this being their third consecutive tournament. A seventh World Cup overall, Tunisia are yet to make it out of the group stage. And they’ve only won three of their 18 World Cup matches so far.

This will be the fifth meeting between these two sides, and Tunisia did win the last matchup. But that came in 2003, and was their first victory over Sweden. The European nation have won two games, with the first-ever meeting in 1976 finishing level.

Graham Potter has settled on a back three after taking charge of Sweden. And there’s no reason for him to change now. A lot of his positions

# Sweden vs. Tunisia live stream: How to watch FIFA World Cup, odds, prediction, pick, lineups, channel

## Graham Potter’s flexible Swedish attack faces a major test against Sabri Lamouchi’s lock-down Tunisian defense in an intriguing Group F opener

21 hrs ago • 3 min read

Add CBS Sports on Google

Getty Images

The group stage is officially underway at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Group F taps into the action with a meeting between Sweden and Tunisia at Estadio BBVA in Monterrey, Mexico, on Sunday.

Sweden are back in the tournament after failing to qualify in 2022, and Tunisia return for their third consecutive World Cup (seven all-time) as both sides aim to make a statement in the group. Sweden, led by English manager Graham Potter, are flexible in attack. The Europeans have a bit of a chip on their shoulders after qualifying for the 2026 World Cup on a lifeline. The

# Team News: Sweden vs. Tunisia injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Ben Knapton| 14 Jun 2026 02:00, Last updated: 14 Jun 2026 02:00

© Imago / TT

Sweden and Tunisia's paths to the 2026 World Cup could hardly have been more contrasting, but their methods of qualification will matter little when their Group F clash gets underway in Guadalupe in the early hours of Monday morning.

Blagult earned their ticket to North America via the playoffs after failing to win a single group game in Qualifying, whereas their African foes earned nine preliminary victories from a possible nine.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both nations.

---

## SWEDEN vs. TUNISIA

## SWEDEN

Out: None

Doubtful: Gabriel Gudmundsson(illness)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Nordfeldt; Hien, Lindelof, Lagerbielke; Bernhardsson, Kar"}

Published: 2026-06-12T11:41:31+00:00
Source: squawka.com (squawka.com)
Language: en

## Story

Sweden get their 2026 World Cup campaign underway when they face Tunisia on Sunday at 3am (UK time).

Sweden are at the World Cup for the 13th time and are back after being absent in 2022. They reached the quarter-finals on their last appearance, and haven’t failed to make it out of the group stage since 1990. But that does also include more failures to qualify in 1998, 2010 and 2014.

Tunisia, meanwhile, have matched their best run of World Cup appearances with this being their third consecutive tournament. A seventh World Cup overall, Tunisia are yet to make it out of the group stage. And they’ve only won three of their 18 World Cup matches so far.

This will be the fifth meeting between these two sides, and Tunisia did win the last matchup. But that came in 2003, and was their first victory over Sweden. The European nation have won two games, with the first-ever meeting in 1976 finishing level.

Graham Potter has settled on a back three after taking charge of Sweden. And there’s no reason for him to change now. A lot of his positions"}

# Sweden vs. Tunisia live stream: How to watch FIFA World Cup, odds, prediction, pick, lineups, channel

## Graham Potter’s flexible Swedish attack faces a major test against Sabri Lamouchi’s lock-down Tunisian defense in an intriguing Group F opener

21 hrs ago • 3 min read

Add CBS Sports on Google

Getty Images

The group stage is officially underway at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Group F taps into the action with a meeting between Sweden and Tunisia at Estadio BBVA in Monterrey, Mexico, on Sunday.

Sweden are back in the tournament after failing to qualify in 2022, and Tunisia return for their third consecutive World Cup (seven all-time) as both sides aim to make a statement in the group.
